items.criterion: The PR implements the referenced control handoff and does not substitute a different goal, cell, or implementation scope.
All changed files remain inside the handoff allowed_paths and match the declared cell scope.
No forbidden_paths, protected surfaces, production/deployment files, secrets, or external automation are touched without explicit authorization.
The PR honestly declares whether it touches auth, database, workflow, deployment, secrets, branch protection, rulesets, runtime, package, MCP, env, or DB paths.
Validation evidence covers the changed surface and any omitted checks are stated as risks or non-scope.
required_evidence: manual check or N/A explanation when applicable

items.criterion: Tests, gates, or validation paths were not deleted, skipped, narrowed, or weakened to make the PR pass.
Runtime, persistence, package identity, MCP namespace, env vars, DB paths, workflows, and deployment behavior remain unchanged unless the handoff explicitly authorizes them.
The PR does not create unreviewed secret exposure, unsafe external sends, permission escalation, source-text trust elevation, or hidden memory/recovery boundary changes.
The PR does not create competing SSOTs, duplicate canonical control surfaces, or stale status claims inconsistent with repo-local Shirube state.
The PR body, comments, and gate evidence accurately report commands run, exact head, failures, limitations, waivers, and known residual risks.
